Welcome to the Getting Started Guide. Whether you're new to our platform or just need a refresher, this guide will walk you through the steps to get up and running quickly.

Our platform is a powerful tool designed to help you achieve X, Y, and Z. It comes with a range of features to facilitate A, B, and C. This guide will ensure you're up and running in no time.

docs.title: System Requirements
docs.text: Before you get started, make sure your system meets the following requirements:

docs.title: Hardware:
docs.text:
• CPU: Dual-core processor or higher
  • RAM: 4GB minimum (8GB recommended)
  • Disk Space: 1GB of free space for the base installation

docs.title: Software:
docs.text:
• Operating System: macOS 10.15+, Windows 10+, or a Linux-based distribution (Ubuntu 20.04 or newer recommended)
  • Dependencies:
  • Node.js (version 14 or newer)
  • Git (for version control)
  • Python (version 3.6 or higher)

If your system doesn't meet these requirements, you may experience performance issues.

docs.title: Installation Instructions
docs.text: Once you've ensured your system meets the requirements, follow the installation steps for your operating system.

docs.title: For macOS:
docs.text:
1. Open your terminal.
  2. Install using Homebrew:

```bash brew install our-software

docs.title: Sequence Diagrams
docs.text:
Sequence diagrams describe a specific scenario as sequential interactions between multiple objects

sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  Server->>Terminal: Send request
  loop Health
      Terminal->>Terminal: Check for health
  end
  Note right of Terminal: System online
  Terminal-->>Server: Everything is OK
  Terminal->>Database: Request customer data
  Database-->>Terminal: Customer data
